content.story: Fire has been a critical survival tool for millennia. A patrol's ability to quickly build and manage a fire is a fundamental Scouting skill.
content.challenge: Working as a team, safely build a fire capable of boiling water using only the provided materials.
content.description:
*   You have **up to 5 minutes** to gather your provided tinder and kindling and prepare to start the fire.
*   You are encouraged to attempt a friction fire start. You may begin building the fire at any time.
*   You may also use matches. Matches carry a penalty cost (e.g., added time per match).
*   The official time starts either when the first embers appear from a friction start, or when the first match is struck.
*   The maximum allowed time is 15 minutes.
*   Once the water boils over the edge of the container, the game is complete.
*   The fire must be properly extinguished and the station reset before your patrol leaves.
content.notes: Ensure safety boundaries around the fire pit. Friction fire attempts should be closely monitored.
content.references: Consider providing reference literature on bow-drill or flint-and-steel techniques at the station.

content.story: Accidents happen when we least expect them. In an emergency, a patrol must remain calm, assess the situation, and act quickly to administer first aid.
content.challenge: Triage and treat multiple injuries on a simulated victim using standard First Aid procedures.
content.description:
*   Assess the scene for safety before approaching the victim.
*   Identify the injuries through observation and communicating with the victim.
*   Apply the correct bandage, splint, or treatment for each identified injury.
*   Work collaboratively as a patrol.
content.notes: Victim actors should be briefed on their symptoms beforehand.
content.references: Refer to the Scouts BSA Handbook First Aid section.

content.story: Campfires are a time-honored tradition for sharing stories, songs, and laughter.
content.challenge: Perform a short, entertaining skit or song for the judges.
content.description:
*   Your patrol must perform a cohesive skit or song.
*   All patrol members must participate in some capacity.
*   The performance should be appropriate and demonstrate Scout Spirit.
content.notes: Judges should look for creativity, volume, and full patrol involvement.

content.story: A Scout is prepared for the elements. Setting up shelter quickly and correctly is essential for a safe camping trip.
content.challenge: Correctly pitch and then strike a tent as a patrol, wearing designated restrictive gear (e.g., thick gloves or blindfolds for some members) to simulate adverse conditions.
content.description:
*   Work together to assemble the provided tent.
*   Judges will pause the clock to inspect the tent once you declare it finished.
*   If errors are found, the clock resumes until they are corrected.
*   Once approved, disassemble and correctly pack the tent back into its bag.
content.notes: Make sure the tent is completely dry before packing away at the end of the day.

content.story: Hot meals boost morale in the field. Every Scout should know how to properly operate a camp stove and cook a basic meal.
content.challenge: Prepare the stove, mix batter, and successfully cook a pancake.
content.description:
*   The entire patrol must participate in the process.
*   Safely connect and ignite the camp stove.
*   Mix the provided ingredients to create a pancake batter.
*   Cook the pancake thoroughly on both sides without burning it.
content.notes: Ensure stove safety and hygienic food handling practices.

content.story: Navigation is a core Scouting skill. Knowing how to find your bearings ensures you can always find your way home.
content.challenge: Navigate an established compass course by following a series of bearings and distances.
content.description:
*   Start at the designated starting marker.
*   Use your compass to follow the provided bearings and distances.
*   Record the identifier found at each destination marker.
*   Return your completed scorecard to the judge.
content.notes: Course markers should be clearly visible but require accurate bearings to locate.

content.story: Respect for the flag is a hallmark of Scouting. Performing a proper flag ceremony demonstrates discipline and patriotism.
content.challenge: Execute a formal flag raising and lowering ceremony.
content.description:
*   The patrol will perform a full color guard ceremony (raising the flags).
*   Following the raising, the patrol will perform the closure (lowering and folding the flags).
*   Attention to detail, commands, and respect are paramount.
content.notes: Ensure the flags do not touch the ground during the exercise.

content.story: Working in unison requires communication and coordination. A patrol that moves as one can overcome any obstacle.
content.challenge: Navigate a specific course while physically linked together as a patrol.
content.description:
*   Patrol members must stand in a circle facing outwards.
*   Adjacent legs will be bound together using bungee cords or bandanas.
*   The patrol must navigate the marked course and return to the start line as many times as possible within the time limit.
content.notes: Monitor for safety to prevent tripping and falling hazards.

content.story: In wilderness rescue scenarios, a patrol may need to transport an injured member over rough terrain using improvised methods.
content.challenge: Construct a rescue stretcher from provided materials and transport a patrol member across the course.
content.description:
*   Build a sturdy stretcher using the provided staves and blankets/ropes.
*   Safely load one patrol member onto the stretcher.
*   Transport the "victim" from the start line to the finish line without dropping them.
*   Disassemble the stretcher and return to the start.
*   Repeat the process. Different patrol members must take different roles on each trip.
*   **NO RUNNING.** Walk with purpose and prioritize safety.
content.notes: Safety is the primary concern. Stop the activity immediately if the stretcher appears unstable.

content.story: Pioneering skills allow Scouts to build structures to overcome obstacles. Lashings are the building blocks of pioneering.
content.challenge: Construct a sturdy ladder using staves and square lashings.
content.description:
*   The entire patrol should participate.
*   Upon the start signal, lash the structural rungs to the main poles using square lashings.
*   The rungs must be spaced evenly and securely fastened.
*   The ladder must hold the weight of a patrol member.
content.notes: Check lashings for tightness and proper fraps.
content.references: Pioneering Merit Badge Pamphlet, square lashings.

content.story: Moving heavy objects over land requires ingenuity. Rollers are an ancient and effective method of transportation.
content.challenge: Transport the entire patrol across a designated area using a wooden platform and rolling barrels.
content.description:
*   A minimum of 4 Scouts must be on the "raft" (plywood platform) at all times.
*   The patrol must move the raft forward by rolling it over the provided barrels.
*   As a barrel rolls out from the back, a Scout must retrieve it and place it squarely in the front path.
*   No part of the raft or the Scouts on it may touch the ground.
content.notes: Ensure the rolling surface is relatively flat and free of major debris.

content.story: Traversing hazardous terrain requires careful stepping and resource management.
content.challenge: Move the entire patrol across the "lava" using only the provided stepping stones (carpet squares).
content.description:
*   The ground is impassable. You may only step on the provided blocks/squares.
*   Each member receives a "stepping stone".
*   As long as a patrol member maintains physical contact with a stone, it remains safe.
*   If a stone is left completely untouched, it is "lost" and removed by the judge.
*   Work together to bridge the gap and move everyone to the safe zone.
content.notes: Judges must be diligent in removing untouched squares immediately.

content.story: Observation and memory are vital skills for tracking, nature study, and situational awareness.
content.challenge: Observe a collection of items for a limited time and then recall as many as possible.
content.description:
*   Inform the judge when your patrol is ready.
*   The judge will reveal a collection of hidden items for exactly 60 seconds (or designated time).
*   Observe the items silently. You may not take notes or photos.
*   Once the items are covered, work as a patrol to list as many items as you can remember.
content.notes: Ensure the items are completely hidden before the patrol arrives. Have a standardized grading sheet.
